jokes.text: Multiple characters bumping into each other, dropping donuts on ground, then being told to eat dirty food
jokes.setup: Characters fighting over donuts for French class
jokes.punchline: Donuts get dirty from collision, but they're told to eat them anyway
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with mild escalation. Gets the job done but predictable.

jokes.text: MY PROBLEM IS YOUR FACE, DIRTBAG.
jokes.setup: Character asking 'What's your problem?'
jokes.punchline: Blunt, harsh response about their face
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical insult that establishes Kim's harsh character perfectly. Generic insult elevated by delivery timing.

jokes.text: WELL, IF SHE'S LOOKING FOR NEW CUSTOMERS, I'M UP FOR A TEST DRIVE.
jokes.setup: Kim Kelly being described as on the warpath after breakup
jokes.punchline: Daniel crudely suggesting he'd hook up with her using car metaphor
jokes.explanation: Establishes Daniel's crude character through metaphor. Slightly cheap but fits the teenage boy dynamic.

jokes.text: JEEZ, PERRY, YOU'RE LOOKING HUGE.
jokes.setup: Perry claiming he's been working out
jokes.punchline: Blunt observation about his increased size
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about teenage body changes. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: THAT GUY HIT YOU IN THE SOLAR PLEXUS.
jokes.setup: Sam saying he can't breathe after being hit
jokes.punchline: Neal's overly clinical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Neal being overly intellectual about simple situation. Shows his nerdy knowledge.

jokes.text: I PROPOSE AFTER SCHOOL WE MAKE SOME ACID, POUR IT ON THE SIDEWALK, AND WATCH IT KILL BUGS.
jokes.setup: Neal excited about chemistry set from his brother
jokes.punchline: His innocent but morbid plan to torture insects
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Neal's scientific interests mixed with boyish cruelty. Memorable and specific.

jokes.text: I DON'T WANNA HEAR YOUR LIFE STORY, OK? I WANT YOU TO MOVE YOUR ASS!
jokes.setup: Sam politely explaining why he's confused about lockers
jokes.punchline: Kim's disproportionately harsh response to innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim Kelly character moment - unnecessarily cruel to innocent Sam. Sharp escalation from politeness to hostility.

jokes.text: YOU ARE VERY CONFUSED, PEE-WEE. ARE YOU SURE YOU'RE EVEN IN THE RIGHT SCHOOL?
jokes.setup: Sam being confused about locker location
jokes.punchline: Kim suggesting he's so confused he might be in wrong school entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kim's bullying. The 'wrong school' suggestion is absurd enough to be funny while maintaining her cruelty.

jokes.text: YOU CAN'T BE 14, MIDGET. YOU HAVEN'T EVEN HIT PUBERTY.
jokes.setup: Sam claiming he's 14 years old
jokes.punchline: Kim publicly questioning his development
jokes.explanation: Peak high school cringe - public humiliation about puberty. Effective discomfort humor that establishes the show's tone.

jokes.text: Kim checking Sam's armpit for hair in front of everyone
jokes.setup: Kim questioning whether Sam has hit puberty
jokes.punchline: She physically inspects his armpit for hair publicly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy. Physical violation that's both shocking and perfectly in character for Kim. Establishes the show's unflinching look at teen humiliation.

jokes.text: YOU DON'T EVEN HAVE HAIR IN YOUR PITS.
jokes.setup: Kim inspecting Sam's armpit
jokes.punchline: Her blunt announcement of the humiliating discovery
jokes.explanation: The verbal follow-up to the physical humiliation. Kim's bluntness makes the cringe even worse.

jokes.text: THEN YOU'RE A GEEK.
jokes.setup: Sam insisting he's 14 despite no armpit hair
jokes.punchline: Kim's matter-of-fact classification of him as a geek
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and logic. Kim's brutal but simple reasoning. Sets up the episode's central theme.

jokes.text: Kim writing 'GEEK' on Sam's locker and explaining it will help him remember which is his
jokes.setup: Sam needing to remember which locker is his
jokes.punchline: Kim's 'helpful' solution that's actually cruel vandalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - framing cruel vandalism as helpful advice. Kim's twisted logic is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: YOU DON'T HAVE HAIR IN YOUR PITS?
jokes.setup: Bill approaching Sam after the humiliating incident
jokes.punchline: His incredulous question highlighting Sam's embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that extends the humiliation. Bill's innocent amazement makes it worse for Sam.

jokes.text: SHE'S A SLUT, OBVIOUSLY. / OBVIOUSLY.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Kim is prudish
jokes.punchline: Kim matter-of-factly calling herself a slut, Daniel agreeing
jokes.explanation: Shocking bluntness that subverts expectations. Kim's self-deprecation and Daniel's casual agreement is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: WHO WALKED IN ON WHO? / GOD, PEOPLE ARE SO GOSSIPY AT THIS SCHOOL.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking for clarification about the gossip
jokes.punchline: Kim immediately complaining about gossip while actively participating
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Kim being hypocritical about gossip while gossiping. Shows her lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: OH. BUT HE'S SICK. HE HAS IMPETIGO.
jokes.setup: Lindsay told Gary Ross' seat is taken
jokes.punchline: The gross medical reason he's absent
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor typical of teen settings. The specific disease name adds authenticity.

jokes.text: JUST BECAUSE SHE'S ALWAYS ON HER PERIOD, YOU KNOW?
jokes.setup: Nick explaining Kim's bad behavior
jokes.punchline: His clueless biological explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic clueless teenage boy explanation. Nick's well-meaning but ignorant comment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: A DEFACED LOCKER... A HUMILIATED FRESHMAN... AND A BUSTY SUCCUBUS FROM HELL--PERHAPS WE'VE LEFT McKINLEY HIGH AND ENTERED THE CHILLING CORRIDORS OF THE TWILIGHT ZONE.
jokes.setup: Neal observing the chaotic situation at school
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic Twilight Zone parody narration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Neal's dramatic flair and pop culture knowledge. The 'busty succubus' description is particularly funny.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, I THOUGHT SHE WAS GONNA MAKE SWEET LOVE TO YA.
jokes.setup: Discussing Kim's armpit inspection of Sam
jokes.punchline: Bill's innocent but absurd misinterpretation as romantic
jokes.explanation: Perfectly innocent Bill misreading the humiliating situation. The phrase 'make sweet love' from a naive kid is hilarious.

jokes.text: MY DAD HAS A RULE: YOU DON'T PISS YOUR PANTS AT SCHOOL.
jokes.setup: Sam mentioning his dad's rule about not fighting girls
jokes.punchline: Neal's dad having a rule about not wetting yourself at school
jokes.explanation: Great one-liner that reveals Neal's dad gives more practical advice. The contrast with Sam's noble rule is perfect.

jokes.text: HEY, I DIDN'T PISS MY PANTS!
jokes.setup: Neal's comment about pissing pants
jokes.punchline: Sam's defensive denial implying he might have
jokes.explanation: Sam's overly defensive reaction makes us wonder if he actually did. Good cringe continuation.

jokes.text: WHAT WAS THAT, BILBO BAGGINS?
jokes.setup: Sam muttering under his breath
jokes.punchline: Kim comparing him to the small Hobbit character
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture insult for Sam's size. Shows Kim can be clever with her cruelty.

jokes.text: ARE YOU GUYS DEFORMED OR SOMETHING? I MEAN, WHAT'S THE DEAL?
jokes.setup: Kim looking at the group of geeks
jokes.punchline: Her blunt question about their appearance/social status
jokes.explanation: Kim's brutal directness about their social status. Mean but funny in its bluntness.

jokes.text: THIS TIME... IN GEEK BLOOD.
jokes.setup: Kim threatening to mark the locker again
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic threat to use Sam's blood as ink
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that's both threatening and absurd. Kim's dramatic flair makes it funny rather than truly scary.

jokes.text: I, UH, I HOPE SHE MEANT YOUR GEEK BLOOD.
jokes.setup: Kim's threat about using geek blood
jokes.punchline: Bill's innocent hope that Sam will be the victim, not him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - innocent self-preservation disguised as concern. Shows the geeks' realistic fear.

jokes.text: GOD, WOULD YOU QUIT YELLING AT ME, YOU PSYCHOPATH?!
jokes.setup: Kim yelling at her mother on the phone
jokes.punchline: Calling her mother a psychopath while yelling herself
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Kim being as aggressive as she accuses her mother of being. Shows family dysfunction.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, SOMETIMES RELATIONSHIPS CAN BE A DRAG. ESPECIALLY IF YOU'RE GOIN' OUT WITH HER.
jokes.setup: Kim screaming at her mother on phone
jokes.punchline: Daniel's dry observation about dating Kim specifically
jokes.explanation: Daniel's characteristic understatement about an obvious situation. Good timing after Kim's outburst.

jokes.text: WELL, AS USUAL, MY MOTHER IS BEING A TOTAL SHREW.
jokes.setup: Kim hanging up from screaming phone fight
jokes.punchline: Her calling her mother names while acting exactly the same way
jokes.explanation: Kim's lack of self-awareness about inheriting her mother's behavior. Solid character consistency.

jokes.text: SHE FORNICATES IT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking what Kim 'does' in the darkroom
jokes.punchline: Millie's clinical/formal way of saying she has sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Millie - using overly formal language for crude topics. 'Fornicates it' is hilariously awkward phrasing.

jokes.text: 'She fornicates it.'
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks what Kim 'does' in the darkroom
jokes.punchline: Millie's clinical, awkward euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Millie moment - makes sex sound like a science experiment. 'Fornicates it' is inspired.

jokes.text: WE CAN'T ALL BE GIRL SCOUTS, MILLIE.
jokes.setup: Millie judging Kim's alleged sexual behavior
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's sarcastic defense using Girl Scout imagery
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Millie's wholesomeness and Kim's reputation. Lindsay finding her voice.

jokes.text: WHAT'S A PYGMY? / IT'S A DWARF FROM AUSTRALIA.
jokes.setup: Sam reading graffiti on his locker
jokes.punchline: Neal's confident but wrong geographical answer
jokes.explanation: Neal being confidently wrong about basic geography. Shows his know-it-all personality with flaws.

jokes.text: IF YOU DON'T GET RID OF THAT, YOU'RE GONNA BE CALLED PYGMY GEEK FOR THE NEXT 4 YEARS.
jokes.setup: Sam's locker having more graffiti added
jokes.punchline: Neal's dire prediction about nickname permanence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how high school reputations stick. Neal understanding the social dynamics.

jokes.text: 'This wasn't you?' / 'Well, yeah, but--' / 'You kids don't respect school property.'
jokes.setup: VP questions Sam about graffiti
jokes.punchline: Sam admits he cleaned it off but still gets lectured about vandalism
jokes.explanation: Classic adult logic failure - punishing the victim who tried to fix the problem.

jokes.text: DID NICK SAY SOMETHING TO YOU? / WELL...YEAH.
jokes.setup: Kim asking if Nick told Lindsay to be nice
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's honest admission ruining the gesture
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's honesty undermining a potentially nice moment. Good character beat showing her directness.

jokes.text: YOU WANT ME TO STAY A MIDGET? IS THAT WHAT YOU WANT?
jokes.setup: Mom commenting on Sam eating too much
jokes.punchline: Sam's dramatic accusation about growth sabotage
jokes.explanation: Great Sam moment - teenage logic about growth and paranoia about his size. Perfectly dramatic overreaction.

jokes.text: I'M MALNOURISHED.
jokes.setup: Sam claiming there's no food in the house
jokes.punchline: His self-diagnosis of malnutrition
jokes.explanation: Sam's continued dramatic interpretation of normal teenage hunger. Good escalation of his size anxiety.

jokes.text: KIM KELLY'S A PSYCHO. SHE AND KAREN SCARFOLI? WELL, UH... THEY'RE VIOLENT.
jokes.setup: Parents asking about Kim Kelly
jokes.punchline: Sam's dramatic but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Sam's earnest warning to his parents. Funny because it's actually accurate description of Kim's behavior.

jokes.text: SHE DOESN'T WEAR FRILLY DRESSES OR PRANCE AROUND LIKE A CHEERLEADER.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending Kim to her parents
jokes.punchline: Her focus on superficial differences rather than behavior
jokes.explanation: Lindsay missing the point about Kim's problematic behavior. Shows her naive perspective on rebellion.

jokes.text: WELL, MAYBE YOU ARE A GEEK.
jokes.setup: Sam complaining about being called a geek
jokes.punchline: Lindsay casually agreeing before realizing what she said
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling moment - Lindsay accidentally being brutally honest. The immediate regret makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I GOT THE WORST CRAMPS. I JUST ABOUT DIED ON THE WAY OVER HERE.
jokes.setup: Kim arriving at Lindsay's house
jokes.punchline: Her immediate oversharing about menstrual cramps
jokes.explanation: Classic Kim oversharing inappropriate details. Establishes her comfort with TMI and lack of social filters.

jokes.text: I HATE LINDSAY AND HER STUPID FRIENDS. / YOU DON'T HATE YOUR SISTER. / YEAH, I DO. SHE SUCKS.
jokes.setup: Sam expressing frustration with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His blunt confirmation despite mother's correction
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry moment. Sam's matter-of-fact hatred is funny in its childish directness.

jokes.text: SHE DOINKED RYAN O'NEAL ONCE AT A PARTY.
jokes.setup: Kim describing her cool aunt Kathy
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of celebrity hookup using 'doinked'
jokes.explanation: Kim's crude but casual way of describing sex with a celebrity. The word 'doinked' is perfectly chosen for her character.

jokes.text: MY PARENTS HATE EVERYBODY. THEY HATE DANIEL. THEY CAN'T STAND KAREN. THE ONLY PERSON THAT THEY LIKE IS YOU.
jokes.setup: Kim explaining her parents' attitude
jokes.punchline: The irony that they like Lindsay, who they've never met
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Kim's deception. The irony of liking someone they've never met based on lies.

jokes.text: LAST WEEKEND, WE WENT UP TO YOUR VACATION HOUSE IN BENTON HARBOR AND WENT WATER-SKIING.
jokes.setup: Kim revealing she's lied to her parents about Lindsay
jokes.punchline: The specific elaborate fake activities she's invented
jokes.explanation: Kim's elaborate fabrications show how desperately she wants to impress her parents. Specific details make it funnier.

jokes.text: I GOT ONE 'D,' OK? I TOLD YOU MY HISTORY TEACHER WAS A PERVERT.
jokes.setup: Parents criticizing Kim's grades
jokes.punchline: Kim's excuse involving inappropriate teacher behavior
jokes.explanation: Kim deflecting responsibility with dramatic excuse. Shows her tendency to blame others for her problems.

jokes.text: AT LEAST I KNOW HOW TO READ.
jokes.setup: Family argument about Kim's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Kim's defensive comeback about basic literacy
jokes.explanation: Kim's defensive response reveals the low bar for achievement in her household. Sad but funny.

jokes.text: MY DAD ALWAYS SAYS A FARMER NEVER REVEALS HOW MANY COWS HE'S GOT.
jokes.setup: Being pressured to reveal family wealth
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's random folksy saying as deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward deflection using random wisdom. The farming metaphor is absurd but delivered seriously.

jokes.text: REALLY? CAN HE GET US A DISCOUNT?
jokes.setup: Learning Lindsay's dad owns a sporting goods store
jokes.punchline: Immediate interest in personal benefit
jokes.explanation: Typical opportunistic response from working-class character. Shows his priorities and social awkwardness.

jokes.text: I LOVE RELIEVIN' MYSELF BEHIND A SHOWER CURTAIN.
jokes.setup: Talking about home remodeling taking time
jokes.punchline: His crude bathroom situation overshare
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - inappropriate bathroom TMI at dinner table. Shows the family's lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: THIS KID JUST GOT A LITTLE BIT ON HIM AND NOW HE'S GOIN' TO DISNEYLAND FOR HIS LAST HOORAH.
jokes.setup: Bill refusing to hold test tube with acid
jokes.punchline: His darkly optimistic view of flesh-eating chemical victim
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Bill moment - finding the silver lining in chemical burns. The Disneyland detail makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I THINK SHE'S A SEX FIEND.
jokes.setup: Discussing Kim's aggressive behavior
jokes.punchline: Neal's dramatic sexual interpretation
jokes.explanation: Neal's overthinking leading to dramatic sexual conclusion. The term 'sex fiend' is amusingly clinical.

jokes.text: GIRLS DON'T GET HORNY. / THEY DON'T? / ONLY GUYS GET HORNY.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Karen being horny
jokes.punchline: Neal's confident but wrong sexual education
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage boy ignorance about sexuality. Neal being confidently wrong about basic biology.

jokes.text: MAYBE KAREN SCARFOLI'S A GUY.
jokes.setup: Learning only guys get horny according to Neal
jokes.punchline: Bill's logical but absurd conclusion about Karen
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bill logic - following Neal's misinformation to its absurd conclusion. Perfect innocent reasoning.

jokes.text: YEAH? WELL, AT LEAST, I'M NOT A PYGMY.
jokes.setup: Bill calling him Bilbo Baggins
jokes.punchline: Sam using the other locker graffiti as comeback
jokes.explanation: Sam fighting back with the other insult. Shows how the bullying has armed them against each other.

jokes.text: BENTON HARBOR... STREET.
jokes.setup: Being pressed for specific street address of fake vacation home
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's obviously made-up street name
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Lindsay's obvious panic leading to terrible improvisation. The pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: YOU ARE A LYING BRAT! I KNEW IT! I TOLD YOU.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's obviously fake street address
jokes.punchline: Mother immediately calling out the lie
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the building tension. Mother's vindication after being suspicious all along.

jokes.text: NEWS FLASH. YOU'RE NOT MY FATHER, FAT ASS!
jokes.setup: Stepfather trying to ground Kim
jokes.punchline: Kim's brutal rejection of his authority with personal attack
jokes.explanation: Kim's nuclear option - rejecting stepfather's authority with cruel body shaming. Escalates the chaos perfectly.

jokes.text: BENTON HARBOR STREET?
jokes.setup: Kim processing what happened at dinner
jokes.punchline: Her incredulous repetition of Lindsay's bad lie
jokes.explanation: Good callback highlighting how bad Lindsay's improvisation was. Kim's disbelief is justified.

jokes.text: SORRY TO BREAK IT TO YOU, BUT YOU'RE THE GEEK.
jokes.setup: Sam and Neal arguing about who's the bigger geek
jokes.punchline: Bill declaring Sam the winner
jokes.explanation: Bill delivering harsh but honest judgment. Good callback to the episode's central geek theme.

jokes.text: ADULTS PLAY WITH CHEMISTRY SETS, TOO. THEY'RE CALLED SCIENTISTS.
jokes.setup: Being mocked for chemistry set interest
jokes.punchline: Sam's defensive but logical comeback
jokes.explanation: Good Sam comeback showing his intelligence. Logical defense that actually makes sense.

jokes.text: MAY I DIRECT THE JURY'S ATTENTION TO EXHIBIT 'A'?
jokes.setup: Sam denying he plays with Tonka trucks
jokes.punchline: Bill's dramatic legal language while producing evidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bill moment using legal drama language for toy argument. The formality makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: WHAT'S GEEK MEAN ANYWAY? I MEAN, IT'S JUST-- YOU KNOW, IT'S JUST A WORD.
jokes.setup: Being forced to choose who's the bigger geek
jokes.punchline: Bill's philosophical deflection about the meaninglessness of labels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill wisdom - trying to rise above the pettiness with philosophy. Shows his thoughtful nature.

jokes.text: THERE ARE A LOT OF FAMOUS GEEKS IN HISTORY-- EINSTEIN, GILLIGAN, GOMER PYLE.
jokes.setup: Sam being declared the geek
jokes.punchline: Neal's bizarre mix of genius and TV characters as examples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Neal logic mixing Einstein with sitcom characters. Shows his pop culture-addled perspective on intelligence.

jokes.text: YOU LOOK FAMILIAR. DOES ANY OF YOUR FAMILY SHOP AT MY STORE? / YEAH, BUT MY MOM HATES GOING THERE 'CAUSE IT'S SO OVERPRICED. YOU KNOW, YOU CAN GET THE SAME STUFF AT SPORTSMART FOR A LOT LESS.
jokes.setup: Harold trying to make friendly conversation
jokes.punchline: Kim brutally insulting his business to his face
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim Kelly moment - completely oblivious to social norms. The detailed comparison to competitor makes it worse.

jokes.text: I THINK HE'S STONED.
jokes.setup: Harold observing Sam's weird behavior
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact conclusion about drug use
jokes.explanation: Harold's practical dad assessment of Sam's strange behavior. Funny because it's wrong but reasonable.

jokes.text: AND AFTER I BREAK HER ARMS, I'M GOING TO TAKE A WRENCH, AND I'M GOING TO PULL OUT HER TEETH ONE BY ONE.
jokes.setup: Kim promising to break Karen's arms
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly elaborate torture plans
jokes.explanation: Kim's escalating violence fantasy becoming cartoonishly elaborate. The specific details make it both scary and funny.

jokes.text: DID YOU DO THIS? / I DID IT. / BECAUSE YOU ARE A SLUT, SLUT.
jokes.setup: Kim confronting Karen about new graffiti
jokes.punchline: Kim's matter-of-fact confession and brutal insult
jokes.explanation: Kim delivering on her revenge promise with characteristic bluntness. The double 'slut' emphasizes her anger.

jokes.text: HEY, KIM, THANKS. THAT WAS REALLY COOL. / NO PROBLEM, GEEK.
jokes.setup: Sam thanking Kim for defending him
jokes.punchline: Kim accepting thanks while still calling him a geek
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Kim's complexity - she helps Sam but maintains the social hierarchy. Great character consistency.
